title: Fatehpur Sikri
tagline: Fatehpur Sikri — heritage city of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Fatehpur Sikri (Hindi: [ˈfətɛɦpʊɾ ˈsiːkɾiː]) is a town in the Agra District of Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Fatehpur Sikri (Hindi: [ˈfətɛɦpʊɾ ˈsiːkɾiː]) is a town in the Agra District of Uttar Pradesh, India. Situated 35.7 kilometres (22.2 mi) from the district headquarters of Agra, Fatehpur Sikri itself was founded as the capital of the Mughal Empire in 1571 by Emperor Akbar, serving this role from 1571 to 1585, when Akbar abandoned it due to a campaign in Punjab and was later completely abandoned in 1610. The name of the city is derived from the village called Sikri which previously occupied the location. An Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) excavation from 1999 to 2000 indicated that there was

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Buland Darwaza
topPlaces.description: Buland Darwaza (lit. 'Exalted Gate'), or the "Door of victory", construction was started in 1573 by Mughal emperor Akbar to commemorate his victory over Gujarat. It is the main entrance to the Jama Masjid at Fatehpur Sikri, which is 43 km from Agra, India.

topPlaces.name: Jama Masjid, Fatehpur Sikri
topPlaces.description: The Jama Mosque, also known as the Jama Masjid, is a 16th-century Sufi Friday mosque and dargah complex, in the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Fatehpur Sikri, located in the Agra district of the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by Mughal emperor Akbar, and was the largest mosque in the empire at the time of construction.

topPlaces.name: Jodha Bai Mahal
topPlaces.description: 'Jodha Bai Mahal' also known incorrectly as 'Jodh Bai Mahal' is the largest palace in Fatehpur Sikri commissioned by Mughal Emperor Akbar in 1569 for his favourite queen, Mariam-uz-Zamani, commonly known as 'Jodha bai' . This Mahal is the largest complex of the zenana (palace for women belonging to the royal household).

topPlaces.name: Birbal Mahal
topPlaces.description: Birbal Mahal (tl. Birbal's Palace) is a palace commisoned by Emperor Akbar in 1571.

topPlaces.name: Sunehra Makan Mahal
topPlaces.description: Sunehra Makan Mahal (The Golden Palace) is a palace located in the harem quarters of the Fatehpur Sikri fort. The palace was named as such because of its rich frescoes and gilded walls.

topPlaces.name: Anup Talao
topPlaces.description: The Anup Talao is a pavilion completed in 1576. It is set in the centre of a pool in the courtyard of the Fatehpur Sikri Fort in Fatehpur Sikri, Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Doora, Agra
topPlaces.description: Doora is a village in the Kiraoli Tehsil of Agra district, Uttar Pradesh, India with a total population of 7,465 as of 2011.

items.note: Buland Darwaza (lit. 'Exalted Gate'), or the "Door of victory", construction was started in 1573 by Mughal emperor Akbar to commemorate his victory over Gujarat. It is the main entrance to the Jama Masjid at Fatehpur Sikri, which is 43 km from Agra, India.

The Jama Mosque, also known as the Jama Masjid, is a 16th-century Sufi Friday mosque and dargah complex, in the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Fatehpur Sikri, located in the Agra district of the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by Mughal emperor Akbar, and was the largest mosque in the empire at the time of construction.

'Jodha Bai Mahal' also known incorrectly as 'Jodh Bai Mahal' is the largest palace in Fatehpur Sikri commissioned by Mughal Emperor Akbar in 1569 for his favourite queen, Mariam-uz-Zamani, commonly known as 'Jodha bai' . This Mahal is the largest complex of the zenana (palace for women belonging to the royal household).
Birbal Mahal (tl. Birbal's Palace) is a palace commisoned by Emperor Akbar in 1571.
Sunehra Makan Mahal (The Golden Palace) is a palace located in the harem quarters of the Fatehpur Sikri fort. The palace was named as such because of its rich frescoes and gilded walls.
The Anup Talao is a pavilion completed in 1576. It is set in the centre of a pool in the courtyard of the Fatehpur Sikri Fort in Fatehpur Sikri, Uttar Pradesh, India.
Doora is a village in the Kiraoli Tehsil of Agra district, Uttar Pradesh, India with a total population of 7,465 as of 2011.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Fatehpur Sikri?
faq.a: The best time to visit Fatehpur Sikri is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Fatehpur Sikri?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (31 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (34 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Fatehpur Sikri?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Fatehpur Sikri at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Fatehpur Sikri?
faq.a: Stays in Fatehpur Sikri start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Fatehpur Sikri?
faq.a: The top attractions are Buland Darwaza, Jama Masjid, Fatehpur Sikri, Jodha Bai Mahal, Birbal Mahal, Sunehra Makan Mahal.
